front: Work: in this novel, the title physician falls in love with the nurse Lara during the Russian Revolution
Author: this Russian author was forced by the USSR to decline the 1958 Nobel Prize in Literature
Work: in this novel, Lara was pressured into a sexual relationship by the corrupt lawyer Komarovsky, whom she later shot at a Christmas party
Work: twenty-five poems including 'Hamlet,' 'Miracle,' and 'The Garden of Gethsemane' are placed at the end of this novel, framed as the title character's work
Work: in the epilogue of this novel, Nika and Mischa meet Tania (the title character's daughter with Lara), who is a day laborer protected by the title character's half-brother Evgraf
Work: 'Hamlet,' a poem placed at the end of this novel, ends with the line 'to live a life is not to cross a field'
Work: the CIA bought and distributed thousands of copies of this novel to make its author a Nobel Prize contender
Work: this was the first full-length novel shared via samizdat
Work: in this novel, the title character is held captive for 18 months by Red Partisans from the Forest Brotherhood, led by Liberius
Work: 'Winter Night,' placed at the end of this novel, repeats the lines 'The candle burns upon the desk, the candle burns'
Work: the title poem of this collection describes life 'overflowing today, / spring rain shattering itself like glass'
Work: this poetry collection opens with 'In Memory of the Demon' and closes with 'The End'
Work: this poetry collection's most famous poem compares 'the timetable on the Kamyshin line' to the Bible
Work: this poetry collection includes the garden and mirror cycles
Author: this poet's most famous poem opens 'February. Take ink and weep'
Author: this poet's inaugural collection Twin in the Clouds was published through the Moscow Futurist group Tsentrifuga
Author: Marina Tsvetaeva was friends with this author and wanted to name her son after him; they also exchanged letters with Rilke in 1926
Author: Osip Mandelstam recited his famous 'Stalin Epigram' at this author's house
Author: this poet wrote translations of many Shakespeare plays using contemporary Russian dialogue
